original_definition: Translate a text written in a newly discovered language into English as well as a team of human experts, using a single other document in both languages (like a Rosetta stone).
evidence_summary: Recent low-resource speech-translation work shows progress, but I did not locate a public demonstration of translating a newly discovered language from a single bilingual document at human-expert level.
strictness_notes: Absence of a located public demonstration is not proof that no private system can do it.

original_definition: Translate speech in a new language given only unlimited films with subtitles in the new language, with training data for other languages.
evidence_summary: Low-resource speech translation has improved, but I did not locate a public system learning a new language solely from unlimited subtitled films plus other-language training data.
strictness_notes: The original training-data condition is unusually specific.

milestone_label: Translate as well as bilingual humans
original_definition: Perform translation about as good as a human fluent in both languages but unskilled at translation, for most text types and popular languages.
evidence_summary: A 2018 Microsoft system reported human parity on a Chinese-to-English news benchmark. A subsequent reassessment argued that parity did not hold under stronger evaluation.
strictness_notes: The original task requires parity for most text types and popular languages, not one news-domain language pair.

milestone_label: Phone bank as well as humans
original_definition: Provide phone banking services as well as human operators can, without annoying customers more than humans.
evidence_summary: Voice agents handle routine banking workflows, but I did not locate a public bank-specific benchmark showing service quality equal to human operators without greater customer annoyance.
strictness_notes: Vendor deployment claims are not a parity benchmark.

original_definition: Correctly group images of previously unseen objects into classes after training on a similar labeled dataset containing completely different classes.
evidence_summary: CLIP demonstrated zero-shot transfer to visual classification benchmarks using category names not seen as task-specific training labels.
strictness_notes: CLIP's natural-language supervision is not identical to the original setup of training on a labeled dataset with completely different classes.

original_definition: See only one labeled image of a new object, then recognize the object in real-world scenes to the extent that a typical human can.
evidence_summary: One-shot image-recognition systems have demonstrated learning from one example, but I did not locate a clean public demonstration matching typical-human recognition in real-world scenes.
strictness_notes: Benchmark one-shot recognition is narrower than robust real-world scene recognition.

original_definition: See a short video of a scene, then construct a 3D model good enough to create a realistic video of the same scene from a substantially different angle.
evidence_summary: NR-NeRF demonstrated dynamic-scene reconstruction and novel-view rendering from monocular video; NeRF established high-quality novel-view synthesis from images.
strictness_notes: The quality threshold 'realistic video' is judgment-based, and the exact capture assumptions matter.

original_definition: Transcribe human speech with a variety of accents in a noisy environment as well as a typical human can.
evidence_summary: Whisper reported approaching human-level robustness and accuracy across diverse speech data.
strictness_notes: Approaching human performance is not the same as proving parity across noisy environments and varied accents.

original_definition: Take a written passage and output a recording that cannot be distinguished from a voice actor by an expert listener.
evidence_summary: NaturalSpeech reported no statistically significant quality difference from human recordings on a benchmark; later work continued to report on-par quality.
strictness_notes: The original test is stricter: indistinguishable from a voice actor to an expert listener.

original_definition: Routinely and autonomously prove mathematical theorems publishable in top mathematics journals today, including generating the theorems to prove.
evidence_summary: Recent autonomous mathematics agents show meaningful research progress, but I did not locate evidence of routinely and autonomously generating and proving top-journal-level theorems.
strictness_notes: One-off assistance or selected open problems is not routine autonomous top-journal mathematics.

original_definition: Perform as well as the best human entrants in the Putnam competition.
evidence_summary: MathArena's Putnam 2025 evaluation reports that leading agentic systems scored at a level that would place within the top ten human competitors.
strictness_notes: The wording 'as well as the best human entrants' can be read as matching the absolute top score, not merely top-ten placement.

original_definition: Defeat the best Go players while training only on as many games as the best Go players have played.
evidence_summary: AlphaGo Zero surpassed prior Go systems through self-play, but it used vastly more games than elite human players have played.
strictness_notes: The low-training-game constraint is the point of this milestone and is not satisfied.

original_definition: Beat the best human Starcraft 2 players at least 50% of the time, given a video of the screen.
evidence_summary: AlphaStar reached Grandmaster level in StarCraft II under human-like interface constraints.
strictness_notes: The original wording specifies receiving a video of the screen. AlphaStar's observation interface does not cleanly match literal pixel-only play.

original_definition: Play a randomly selected computer game, including difficult ones, about as well as a human novice after less than 10 minutes of game time.
evidence_summary: SIMA-family agents show generalization to unseen 3D environments and a narrowing human-performance gap.
strictness_notes: I did not locate a demonstration satisfying random difficult-game selection, novice-level play, and less than ten minutes of experience.

original_definition: Play new levels of Angry Birds better than the best human players.
evidence_summary: The Angry Birds AI competition literature describes substantial remaining weaknesses on deceptive and unfamiliar levels. I did not locate a later public benchmark establishing superhuman performance on new levels.
strictness_notes: A fresh competition result could change this assessment.

milestone_label: Beat professionals at all Atari games
original_definition: Outperform professional game testers on all Atari games using no game-specific knowledge.
evidence_summary: Agent57 was reported as the first deep-RL agent to outperform the standard human benchmark on all 57 Atari games.
strictness_notes: The public benchmark uses human normalized scores; it is the cleanest match among the 32 tasks.

original_definition: Outperform human novices on 50% of Atari games after only 20 minutes of training play time and no game-specific knowledge.
evidence_summary: EfficientZero achieved strong Atari performance with 100k environment steps, approximately two hours of real-time play.
strictness_notes: Two hours is substantially longer than the original twenty-minute training budget.

milestone_label: Fold laundry as well as humans
original_definition: Fold laundry as well and as fast as the median human clothing-store employee.
evidence_summary: Robots can fold constrained garments in demos, but I did not locate evidence matching the speed and reliability of a median clothing-store employee.
strictness_notes: Garment variability and speed remain important gaps.

milestone_label: Beat a human in a 5km race
original_definition: Beat the fastest human runners in a 5 kilometer race through city streets using a bipedal robot body.
evidence_summary: An autonomous Honor humanoid robot reportedly completed the 2026 Beijing E-Town half-marathon in 50:26, faster than the human half-marathon world record.
strictness_notes: This appears stronger than the original five-kilometer city-street requirement. Because the result is recent, verify official race records before publication.

original_definition: Physically assemble any LEGO set given the pieces and instructions, using non-specialized robotics hardware.
evidence_summary: LEGO assembly remains an active robotics challenge. I did not locate a public system capable of physically assembling any LEGO set from pieces and instructions on non-specialized hardware.
strictness_notes: Small-structure demonstrations are much narrower than arbitrary-set assembly.

original_definition: Learn to efficiently sort lists of numbers much larger than any training set used, without being given the form of the solution.
evidence_summary: AlphaDev discovered sorting algorithms from scratch that improved established small-sort routines and were incorporated into standard libraries.
strictness_notes: The original wording emphasizes generalizing to lists much larger than training sets; AlphaDev is a close but not literal match.

original_definition: Write concise, efficient, human-readable Python code to implement simple algorithms such as quicksort.
evidence_summary: Codex demonstrated generation of Python functions from natural-language descriptions on HumanEval, including simple algorithmic tasks.
strictness_notes: Conciseness, efficiency, and readability are partly qualitative; later systems are stronger, but 2021 is a defensible earliest marker.

original_definition: Answer any easily Googleable factoid question posed in natural language better than an expert on the relevant topic with internet access.
evidence_summary: Modern language models answer many open-domain factoid questions well, but I did not locate a clean benchmark proving performance better than a topic expert with internet access for any easily Googleable factoid.
strictness_notes: The expert-with-internet comparator makes the original task unusually demanding.

original_definition: Answer any easily Googleable factual but open-ended question posed in natural language better than an expert on the relevant topic with internet access.
evidence_summary: Deep-research systems can produce sourced answers to open-ended factual questions, but I did not locate a benchmark proving they outperform relevant experts with internet access on any easily Googleable open-ended question.
strictness_notes: The exact universal claim is stronger than product demonstrations.

original_definition: Give good natural-language answers to factual questions for which there are no definite correct answers.
evidence_summary: ChatGPT publicly demonstrated useful natural-language responses to questions without a single definite correct answer.
strictness_notes: The word 'good' is subjective. Use this milestone only if the story explains that the threshold is judgment-based.

milestone_label: High marks for a high school essay
original_definition: Write an essay for a high-school history class that would receive high grades and pass plagiarism detectors.
evidence_summary: Studies found ChatGPT-generated essays could exhibit high originality, evade common plagiarism checks, and receive favorable quality ratings relative to human essays.
strictness_notes: The studies are not an exact replication of a high-school history-class grading exercise.

original_definition: Compose a song good enough to reach the US Top 40; output the complete song as an audio file.
evidence_summary: AI-associated and AI-assisted music has charted, but I did not locate a verified fully AI-generated complete song reaching the main US Top 40.
strictness_notes: Billboard subcharts and AI-assisted releases do not satisfy the literal target.

original_definition: Produce a song indistinguishable from a new song by a particular artist, e.g. Taylor Swift, to experienced listeners.
evidence_summary: AI voice and music imitation are widespread concerns, but I did not locate an experienced-listener study showing a fully generated new song indistinguishable from a specific artist's work.
strictness_notes: Existence of deepfakes is not the same as satisfying the blinded-listener test.

original_definition: Write a novel or short story good enough to make the New York Times best-seller list.
evidence_summary: I did not locate a verified AI-written novel or short story that made the New York Times best-seller list.
strictness_notes: This is an outcome milestone, so the absence of a verified public example is meaningful but should still be monitored.

original_definition: For any computer game that can be played well by a machine, explain the machine's choice of moves concisely and completely to a layperson.
evidence_summary: Explainability research has advanced, but I did not locate a general system that concisely and completely explains a game-playing machine's move choices to laypeople for any game the machine can play well.
strictness_notes: The words 'any', 'concisely', and 'completely' make this a very demanding target.

original_definition: Play poker well enough to win the World Series of Poker.
evidence_summary: Pluribus defeated elite professionals in six-player no-limit Texas Hold'em, a major multiplayer poker format.
strictness_notes: The system did not literally enter and win a World Series of Poker event, so the match is inferential rather than exact.

milestone_label: Output laws of physics of virtual world
original_definition: After spending time in a virtual world, output the differential equations governing that world in symbolic form.
evidence_summary: AI Feynman and follow-on work recovered symbolic equations and ordinary differential-equation systems from data on benchmarks.
strictness_notes: Benchmark symbolic regression is narrower than spending time in an arbitrary virtual world and outputting its governing differential equations.
